description_long: This object is a single, open flower with five broad, rounded petals that display a gradient of deep red to white, radiating outward from a central yellowish-brown stamen cluster. The petals are slightly ruffled at the edges and overlap each other, creating a natural, organic symmetry. The flower's center is prominent, with a textured, pollen-rich appearance, and the petals are thin and delicate, showing subtle veining. The flower lacks a visible stem or leaves, appearing as a detached bloom. The coloration is vivid, with the red hue most intense at the petal tips and fading to white near the center. The overall form is flat and spread out, typical of many ornamental or wild rose varieties.
description: A vivid, five-petaled flower with a red-to-white gradient and a prominent yellowish center, featuring overlapping, ruffled petals.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from above, showing all five petals spread out with a clear view of the yellowish center and the red-to-white gradient.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the thinness of the petals and the layered, slightly curved structure, with the petal edges visible.
description_view_2: Another top-down view, similar to view 0, but with a slightly different angle, highlighting the symmetry and central stamen.
description_view_3: A side view from the opposite direction, showing the curvature and overlapping of the petals, with the red coloration more prominent.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, overlapping slightly at the base and forming a roughly symmetrical, circular pattern typical of many eudicot flowers.
